conversations.say: Maren, we need to set a strict disclosure boundary immediately before anyone treats the Night Desk routing slip as a suspect list.
conversations.offer: Firsthand knowledge of collaborative workflows and our shared history.
conversations.request: Agreement on a disclosure boundary that keeps contributor names out of circulation.

conversations.say: Jules, I hear you want to carry R's mantle. Tell me what binding protections you will guarantee for the contributors whose labor built it.
conversations.offer: Conditional support from the Night Desk network.
conversations.request: Explicit obligations protecting contributor anonymity and preventing private ownership.

conversations.say: Arden, Veridian holds archive access we need, but we will not lend this anniversary legitimacy without concrete source-protection guarantees.
conversations.offer: Bounded workflow evidence from our records.
conversations.request: Formal, durable protections and archive terms for precarious workers.

public_statement: Fifteen years of R was built by hands no single byline can contain. We demand that any reckoning honor the collective labor rather than turning workers into suspects.

commitments.promise: Establish a joint disclosure boundary before discussing contributor details further.

goal_progress: Initiated boundary discussion with Maren to protect anonymous contributors.
Challenged Jules on succession obligations and contributor protections.
Approached Arden for concrete source-protection terms in exchange for bounded workflow evidence.
reasoning_summary: Tessa begins Section 1 focused on securing the Night Desk collective against fallout from R.'s provocative message. She coordinates with Maren to set a disclosure boundary, confronts Jules regarding succession obligations, and presses Arden for formal institutional protections before lending legitimacy to Veridian's narrative.
